subject: [PATCH v3 07/20] ntfs: convert iomap ops to ->iomap_next()

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch converts the iomap operations for NTFS to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, which is generated using macros that wrap the iomap_iter_next() helper. This change does not introduce any functional changes and only updates the internal implementation of the iomap operations. The conversion is done for several functions: ntfs_read_iomap_ops, ntfs_zero_read_iomap_ops, ntfs_seek_iomap_ops, ntfs_write_iomap_ops, ntfs_page_mkwrite_iomap_ops, and ntfs_dio_iomap_ops.

reviews.author: Namjae Jeon
reviews.summary: The reviewer acknowledged the patch and applied their Acked-by tag without raising any concerns or suggestions.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jul 21, 2026 at 6:02 AM Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> Convert ntfs iomap_ops to the new ->iomap_next() callback. Each callback is
> generated with the DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT()/DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END()
> macros, which wrap the iomap_iter_next() helper to finish the previous
> mapping if needed and produce the next one. No functional changes are
> intended.
>
> Signed-off-by: Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com>
Acked-by: Namjae Jeon <linkinjeon@kernel.org>
Thanks!

2026-07-21.patch_summary: This patch series converts the iomap operations for NTFS to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, which is generated using macros that wrap the iomap_iter_next() helper. This change does not introduce any functional changes and only updates the internal implementation of the iomap operations.
